parameters.content:
### New Booking Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Starttime, Service). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Check Calendar Availability

### Check Calendar Availability

Checks the team calendar for existing events in the target window — the availability check that runs before booking.

Next: Slot Free?

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Slot Free?

Two-way gate on `={{ $items().length === 0 || !$json.id }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Create Calendar Event · ②→ Propose Alternative Slots

### Create Calendar Event

Books a new event on the team calendar, once availability has been confirmed upstream.

Next: Send Confirmation (Twilio)

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Send Confirmation (Twilio)

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Next: Hold Until Reminder Time

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Propose Alternative Slots

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Hold Until Reminder Time

Pauses 1 hours — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Send Reminder (Twilio)

### Send Reminder (Twilio)

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Next: Send Summary to Twilio

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

# Book demos with Google Calendar and Twilio

**Pattern:** Meeting Scheduling · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Book demos with Google Calendar and Twilio. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual booking is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Booking Form Submission → Check Calendar Availability → Slot Free? → Create Calendar Event → Send Confirmation (Twilio) → Hold Until Reminder Time → Send Reminder (Twilio) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to meeting scheduling without manual effort
- Anyone automating booking in healthcare
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Improves response time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Availability is checked *before* booking, not assumed — creating an event unconditionally would double-book the same slot if two requests arrive close together. The confirmation and the later reminder are separate sends because they serve different moments: confirming intent now, and cutting no-shows right before the appointment.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Change the reminder lead time from 1 hour to 24 hours.
2. Simulate two near-simultaneous booking requests for the same slot and see which gets the alternative-slots branch.
3. Add a cancellation path that frees the slot back up.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your booking process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
